description:
Upon BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1, the BMS shall:
  · Broadcast BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 on CAN ID 0x180 at 100 ms periodicity
  · Reduce charge current target to 50 % of nominal
supplementary_info:
Function: Overvoltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating
Context: When a confirmed overvoltage fault is present, the BMS initiates a controlled derating phase before resorting to contactor-level actions. Warning signals are broadcast on CAN for VCU coordination:
- BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1: overvoltage confirmed (BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1)
  Transmission: CAN ID 0x180, periodicity 100 ms
- BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1: charge termination requested
- Charge current target reduced to 50 % of nominal during derating

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1 -> BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1, Charge_current_target reduced to 50 % of nominal
missing_critical_info: none

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior when BMS_CellOV_Flag transitions from 0 to 1 and triggers the broadcast of BMS_CellOV_Warn on CAN ID 0x180 with periodicity of 100 ms, and reduction of charge current target to 50 % of nominal.
Test the behavior when BMS_CellOV_Flag is at the boundary between 0 and 1, ensuring that the transition triggers the correct response without false positives or negatives.
Test the fault condition where BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1 for a duration long enough to trigger the warning broadcast and charge current reduction.
Test the state transition from normal operation to derating mode when BMS_CellOV_Flag transitions from 0 to 1, ensuring all related signals change as expected.

cases.title: Test the normal behavior when BMS_CellOV_Flag transitions from 0 to 1 and triggers CAN warning broadcast with periodicity of 100 ms, and reduction of charge current target to 50 % of nominal.
cases.objective: To verify that upon BMS_CellOV_Flag transitioning from 0 to 1, the BMS broadcasts BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 on CAN ID 0x180 with periodicity of 100 ms and reduces charge current target to 50 % of nominal.
cases.precondition: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults, BMS_CellOV_Flag = 0, BMS_CellOV_Warn = 0, Charge_current_target at nominal value, BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 0.
cases.postcondition: BMS returned to normal operating state with BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1, BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1, Charge_current_target at 50 % of nominal, BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 0.

steps.action: Set BMS_CellOV_Flag to 1
steps.expected: BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1, BMS_CellOV_Warn = 0 (initial state), Charge_current_target at nominal value, BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 0.

steps.action: Wait for t_Broadcast_Periodicity = 100 ms
steps.expected: BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1, BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 (broadcast on CAN ID 0x180), Charge_current_target reduced to 50 % of nominal, BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 0.

cases.raw_html:
<testcase>

<related_requirement>REQ-BMS-OVP-005</related_requirement>
<precondition>BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults, BMS_CellOV_Flag = 0, BMS_CellOV_Warn = 0, Charge_current_target at nominal value, BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 0.</precondition>

<expected>BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1, BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 (broadcast on CAN ID 0x180), Charge_current_target reduced to 50 % of nominal, BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 0.</expected>

<postcondition>BMS returned to normal operating state with BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1, BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1, Charge_current_target at 50 % of nominal, BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 0.</postcondition>
</testcase>

description:
IF  calculated SOC <= 20 %
THEN
  · BMS_LowSOC_Warn := 1
  · Command instrument cluster low battery lamp ON
supplementary_info:
Function: SOC/SOH Estimation
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring
Context: SOC monitoring provides the driver with range information and protects the battery from deep discharge by enforcing progressive power limits. The two-level warning scheme distinguishes between driver notification and actionable limitation:
- BMS_LowSOC_Warn = 1: SOC <= 20 % (advisory, instrument cluster lamp ON)
- BMS_LowSOC_Flag = 1: SOC <= 10 % (discharge limited to 25 % of MaxDischargePower)

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_LowSOC_Warn := 1 when calculated SOC <= 20 %
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior of setting BMS_LowSOC_Warn to 1 when the calculated SOC is less than or equal to 20%.
Test the boundary condition where BMS_LowSOC_Warn remains 0 when the calculated SOC is slightly above 20%.
Verify that Command instrument cluster low battery lamp ON is issued when BMS_LowSOC_Warn := 1.
Test the transition from BMS_LowSOC_Warn = 0 to BMS_LowSOC_Warn = 1 as calculated SOC decreases through 20%.
Test the behavior when a sudden drop in SOC occurs, ensuring that BMS_LowSOC_Warn is set correctly and the instrument cluster lamp turns on promptly.

cases.postcondition: BMS returned to normal operating state, BMS_LowSOC_Warn reset to 0 and instrument cluster low battery lamp OFF.

description:
The BMS shall monitor the HV interlock loop continuously.
IF  HVIL circuit is interrupted
THEN  BMS_HVILFault_Flag := 1
  · All HV contactor closure prohibited
supplementary_info:
Function: Insulation & HVIL
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring
Context: The HV interlock loop (HVIL) provides a hardware-based detection path for connector disconnection or harness damage. When the circuit is interrupted, the system must immediately isolate the HV system to prevent exposure to live terminals:
- BMS_HVILFault_Flag = 1: HVIL circuit interrupted
- Response: all contactors open within 100 ms, BMS_SystemState = SafeState

analysis.direction:
triggering: BMS_HVILFault_Flag is set to 1 when the HV interlock loop is interrupted.
clearing: All contactors are opened within 100 ms, and BMS_SystemState transitions to SafeState.
missing_critical_info: none

Ensure that the system transitions to SafeState and all contactors are opened after BMS_HVILFault_Flag is set due to an interruption in the HV interlock loop.

description: The BMS shall monitor each cell voltage against the overvoltage threshold. BMS_CellOV_Detect shall be set to 1 when any cell voltage >= r_CellOV_Threshold.
supplementary_info:
Function: Overvoltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation
Context: Overvoltage protection ensures that cell voltages remain within the safe operating range defined by the cell manufacturer. Detection is performed continuously and follows a three-level escalation scheme:
- BMS_CellOV_Detect = 1: cell voltage >= r_CellOV_Threshold (raw detection)
- BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1: detection debounced for >= t_CellOV_Debounce (confirmed fault)
- BMS_CellOV_L3_Flag = 1: voltage >= r_CellOV_L3_Threshold (critical, contactor opening) | The following calibration parameters define the overvoltage detection thresholds and timing:
- r_CellOV_Threshold: detection level [4.15 V - 4.35 V]
- t_CellOV_Debounce: confirmation debounce time [50 ms - 500 ms]
- r_CellOV_L3_Threshold: critical overvoltage level [4.25 V - 4.40 V]

analysis.direction: BMS_CellOV_Detect shall be set to 1 when any cell voltage >= r_CellOV_Threshold. BMS_CellOV_Flag and BMS_CellOV_L3_Flag are confirmation flags that follow a debounce mechanism.
missing_critical_info: none

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior of setting BMS_CellOV_Detect to 1 when any cell voltage reaches or exceeds r_CellOV_Threshold.
Test that BMS_CellOV_Flag is not set if the cell voltage just meets r_CellOV_Threshold but does not meet t_CellOV_Debounce.
Test that BMS_CellOV_Flag is set when the cell voltage exceeds r_CellOV_Threshold and the debounce time t_CellOV_Debounce has been met.
Test that BMS_CellOV_L3_Flag is not set if the cell voltage just meets r_CellOV_L3_Threshold but does not meet t_CellOV_Debounce.
Test that BMS_CellOV_L3_Flag is set when the cell voltage exceeds r_CellOV_L3_Threshold and the debounce time t_CellOV_Debounce has been met.

description:
IF  calculated SOH <= 80 %
THEN
  · BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn := 1
  · Store DTC P0A0F in non-volatile memory
supplementary_info:
Function: SOC/SOH Estimation
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring
Context: SOH degradation tracking enables early detection of battery aging and supports warranty analysis. Degradation is evaluated through multiple indicators including capacity fade and cumulative throughput. When the design cycle limit is exceeded, an SOH re-calibration is requested:
- BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn = 1: SOH <= 80 %
- BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag = 1: cumulative throughput exceeds limit, DTC P0A0F stored

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn := 1 when SOH <= 80 %
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

Test the fault condition where calculated SOH is significantly below 80% to ensure BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn triggers correctly.
Verify that storing DTC P0A0F in non-volatile memory occurs when the threshold for BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn is met.

description:
IF  max cell voltage delta >= 50 mV AND SOC > 30 %
THEN
  · Activate passive cell balancing
  · Select cells with highest voltage for discharge
supplementary_info:
Function: Cell Balancing
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation
Context: Cell balancing compensates for voltage differences between cells to maximise usable capacity and prevent individual cells from exceeding safe voltage limits. Balancing is only active when the pack SOC is sufficiently high and pack current is low. Each balancing event is logged with start and end timestamps, discharged cells, and delta before and after:
- Activation: max delta >= 50 mV AND SOC > 30 %
- Deactivation: delta < 20 mV or balancing timeout

missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

Test the deactivation of passive cell balancing due to a balancing timeout without any change in cell voltage delta or SOC.
Verify that passive cell balancing is deactivated when max cell voltage delta remains below 20 mV for an extended period, simulating a recovery scenario.

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior when BMS_Fault_Latch is set to 1, ensuring all contactor closure is prohibited and normal operation is blocked until a power cycle.
Verify the system's response when BMS_Fault_Latch transitions from 0 to 1, confirming the immediate prohibition of contactor closure and blocking of normal operation.

description: A current sensor self-test shall be performed during each power-up initialisation. The test shall inject a known reference and verify within 2 % accuracy.
supplementary_info:
Function: Current Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault
Context: Current sensor plausibility is verified against a model-predicted value based on pack voltage slope and operating mode. A sensor fault triggers degraded operation using voltage-based power estimation. Sensor self-test is performed during each power-up initialisation (see section 13):
- BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag = 1: sensor deviation > 10 % from prediction
- BMS_DeratePower_Req = 1: request VCU to reduce power demand
- Fault persisting > 5 s triggers BMS_SafeState_Entry = 1

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag = 1 when sensor deviation > 10 % from prediction; clearing: BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag = 0 after fault is resolved.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Verify that the current sensor self-test is performed during each power-up initialization and the BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag is cleared if the deviation is within 10% accuracy.
Test the boundary condition where the sensor deviation is exactly 10% from the predicted value, ensuring that the BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag is set appropriately.
Simulate a fault scenario where the current sensor deviation exceeds 10%, and verify that BMS_DeratePower_Req is set to 1 and the power demand is reduced by the VCU.
Test the state transition from normal operation to degraded operation when the sensor fault persists for more than 5 seconds, ensuring that BMS_SafeState_Entry = 1 is triggered.
Verify that the current sensor self-test results are observable and can be monitored through the BMS_CurrSenseFault_Flag during power-up initialization.

analysis.direction: The requirement describes a triggering behavior: IF CAN communication restored AND fault cleared THEN resume normal operation.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the BMS behavior when the CAN communication is restored and the fault is cleared, ensuring that the system resumes normal operation.
Verify the BMS response to a CAN communication failure by setting BMS_CANFault_Flag = 1 and monitoring if essential sensor inputs are monitored and basic safety functions are maintained.
Test the transition from fault state back to normal operation, ensuring that all systems resume their normal functions when the CAN communication is restored and the fault is cleared.
Ensure that the BMS can detect a CAN communication failure by setting BMS_CANFault_Flag = 1 and verifying that it triggers appropriate monitoring and safety functions.

description:
IF  total pack voltage < minimum operating threshold
THEN
  · BMS_PackUV_Flag := 1
  · Send DTC P0A01 to VCU
  · Limit discharge power proportionally to undervoltage severity
supplementary_info:
Function: Pack Voltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring
Context: Pack voltage monitoring provides a system-level check that complements individual cell monitoring (section 8). A pack undervoltage or sensor plausibility fault is reported to the VCU with DTC. The pack voltage measurement is cross-checked against the sum of cell voltages to detect sensor faults:
- BMS_PackUV_Flag = 1: pack voltage < minimum operating threshold, DTC P0A01 sent
- BMS_PackVoltSensorFault_Flag = 1: cell-sum to pack measurement deviation > 5 V

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_PackUV_Flag := 1 when total pack voltage < minimum operating threshold.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where the total pack voltage is below the minimum operating threshold, and BMS_PackUV_Flag should be set to 1.
Test the boundary condition where the total pack voltage equals the minimum operating threshold. Verify that BMS_PackUV_Flag remains at 0.
Test a scenario where the cell-sum to pack measurement deviation is greater than 5 V, and verify that BMS_PackVoltSensorFault_Flag is set to 1 while BMS_PackUV_Flag should not be triggered.
Test the transition from normal operation to undervoltage protection state. Verify that BMS_PackUV_Flag transitions correctly when total pack voltage drops below threshold and DTC P0A01 is sent.
Verify the observability of the system by checking that the correct DTC (P0A01) is sent to the VCU when BMS_PackUV_Flag is set due to undervoltage condition.

cases.title: Logging of Balancing Start and End Timestamps During Cell Balancing Event
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS logs the start and end timestamps during a cell balancing event.
cases.precondition: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults.
cases.postcondition: System returned to normal operating state with all parameters within normal range, no active faults.

steps.action: Set the r_CellOV_Threshold to a value that will trigger an overvoltage condition for one of the cells.
steps.expected: No immediate response from BMS as the threshold is not yet exceeded.

steps.action: Simulate an overvoltage condition on the cell by applying a voltage above r_CellOV_Threshold.
steps.expected: The BMS will detect the overvoltage and initiate the balancing process. The flag for overvoltage detection will be set, and the system will begin to balance the cell.

steps.action: Wait until the cell balancing is completed.
steps.expected: [NEEDS REVIEW] The BMS logs the end timestamp of the balancing event. The flag for overvoltage detection will be cleared, and the system returns to normal operating state.

steps.action: Check the log entries for the start and end timestamps of the cell balancing event.
steps.expected: The BMS logs the start timestamp when the overvoltage condition was detected and the end timestamp when the balancing process is completed. The log entries include the cells balanced, discharge duration per cell, and voltage delta before and after balancing as required by REQ-BMS-BAL-003.

cases.raw_html:
<testcase>
<title>Logging of Balancing Start and End Timestamps During Cell Balancing Event</title>
<objective>To verify that the BMS logs the start and end timestamps during a cell balancing event.</objective>
<related_requirement>REQ-BMS-BAL-003</related_requirement>
<precondition>BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults.</precondition>
<steps>
<step order="1">
<action>Set the r_CellOV_Threshold to a value that will trigger an overvoltage condition for one of the cells.</action>
<expected>No immediate response from BMS as the threshold is not yet exceeded.</expected>
</step>
<step order="2">
<action>Simulate an overvoltage condition on the cell by applying a voltage above r_CellOV_Threshold.</action>
<expected>The BMS will detect the overvoltage and initiate the balancing process. The flag for overvoltage detection will be set, and the system will begin to balance the cell.</expected>
</step>
<step order="3">
<action>Wait until the cell balancing is completed.</action>
<expected>[NEEDS REVIEW] The BMS logs the end timestamp of the balancing event. The flag for overvoltage detection will be cleared, and the system returns to normal operating state.</expected>
</step>
<step order="4">
<action>Check the log entries for the start and end timestamps of the cell balancing event.</action>
<expected>The BMS logs the start timestamp when the overvoltage condition was detected and the end timestamp when the balancing process is completed. The log entries include the cells balanced, discharge duration per cell, and voltage delta before and after balancing as required by REQ-BMS-BAL-003.</expected>
</step>
</steps>
<postcondition>System returned to normal operating state with all parameters within normal range, no active faults.</postcondition>
</testcase>

cases.title: Verify logging of cells balanced, discharge duration during balancing event
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS logs the correct cells balanced and their respective discharge durations during a cell balancing event.
cases.precondition: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults.
cases.postcondition: System returned to normal operating state, with all parameters within normal range and no active faults.

steps.action: Set the r_CellOV_Threshold to trigger a balancing event by applying an overvoltage condition on one or more cells.
steps.expected: The BMS shall initiate cell balancing and log the start timestamp for the balancing event. [NEEDS REVIEW] The system shall debounce any false positives before logging the actual start of the balancing event.

steps.action: Simulate a discharge process on the cells involved in the balancing event, ensuring that each cell is discharged for a duration sufficient to observe its behavior.
steps.expected: The BMS shall log the discharge duration for each cell being balanced. [NEEDS REVIEW] The system shall debounce any false positives before logging the actual end of the balancing event.

steps.action: Once the balancing process is complete, apply a command to stop the balancing operation and confirm that the BMS logs the end timestamp for the balancing event.
steps.expected: The BMS shall log the end timestamp for the balancing event. [NEEDS REVIEW] The system shall debounce any false positives before logging the actual end of the balancing event.

description:
IF  unrecoverable internal fault detected
THEN
  · Transition to SafeState
  · Open all contactors
  · BMS_Fault_Latch := 1
  · Broadcast fault status on CAN
supplementary_info:
Function: Fault Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring > 11 Degradation Monitoring > 11.1 Cell Degradation Detection > 12 Fault Management > 12.1 Unrecoverable Fault Handling
Context: Unrecoverable internal faults represent failures from which the BMS cannot safely continue normal operation. These include hardware integrity failures and critical sensor path loss. Recovery from this state requires a power cycle, and the fault code is retained for diagnostic readout:
- Memory error (ECC uncorrectable), critical sensor failure, watchdog timeout
- Response: SafeState, all contactors open, BMS_Fault_Latch = 1, power cycle required

analysis.direction: Triggering: Transition to SafeState, Open all contactors, BMS_Fault_Latch := 1, Broadcast fault status on CAN.

Confirm that the system transitions from normal operation to SafeState after detecting an unrecoverable internal fault.
Check that all relevant signals are updated correctly when an unrecoverable internal fault is detected (BMS_Fault_Latch, contactors state).

description:
IF  any cell temperature sensor >= 55 C
THEN
  · BMS_CellOT_Flag := 1
  · Store DTC in non-volatile memory
supplementary_info:
Function: Thermal Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection
Context: Cell temperature is monitored continuously to prevent thermal damage and maintain safe operating limits. Over-temperature events are classified into escalating response levels, with cross-dependency on overvoltage protection (section 1) for coordinated power reduction:
- BMS_CellOT_Flag = 1: any cell >= 55 C
- BMS_CellOT_L2_Flag = 1: any cell >= 60 C (maximum cooling, power reduction)
- BMS_ChargeDerating_Active = 1: cell > 45 C during charging

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CellOT_Flag := 1 when any cell temperature >= 55 C
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where a single cell temperature reaches 55°C, triggering BMS_CellOT_Flag.
Test the boundary condition where exactly one cell is at 55°C and no other cells are above this threshold.
Test that the system correctly stores DTC in non-volatile memory when BMS_CellOT_Flag is triggered.
Verify that BMS_ChargeDerating_Active remains unchanged if no charging state exists during an over-temperature event.

<title>Test that the system correctly stores DTC in non-volatile memory when BMS_CellOT_Flag is triggered</title>
<objective>To verify that the BMS stores a diagnostic trouble code (DTC) in non-volatile memory upon triggering of the BMS_CellOT_Flag due to cell temperature exceeding r_CellOT_Threshold.</objective>
<related_requirement>REQ-BMS-THM-001</related_requirement>
<precondition>BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults.</precondition>
<steps>
<step order="1">
<action>Simulate a cell temperature sensor reading that is equal to or greater than r_CellOT_Threshold (55 C).</action>
<expected>BMS_CellOT_Flag := 1; Store DTC in non-volatile memory [NEEDS REVIEW: within debounce period t_CellOV_Debounce]</expected>
</step>
</steps>
<postcondition>System returned to normal operating state, with the appropriate DTC stored in non-volatile memory.</postcondition>
</testcase>

cases.title: Verify that BMS_ChargeDerating_Active remains unchanged if no charging state exists during an over-temperature event
cases.objective: To verify the behavior of BMS_CellOT_Flag and BMS_ChargeDerating_Active under conditions where cell temperature exceeds r_CellOT_Threshold but no charging is active.
cases.precondition: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults. Cell temperature sensors report a value >= r_CellOT_Threshold (55 C).
cases.postcondition: BMS returned to normal operating state with no active faults, and BMS_ChargeDerating_Active remains unchanged from its initial state.

steps.action: Simulate cell temperature sensor reading to be >= r_CellOT_Threshold.
steps.expected: BMS_CellOT_Flag := 1

steps.action: Wait for t_CellOV_Debounce duration (debounce period).
steps.expected: No change in BMS_ChargeDerating_Active state.

steps.action: Verify that BMS_ChargeDerating_Active remains unchanged from its initial state.
steps.expected: BMS_ChargeDerating_Active := 0 (unchanged)

description:
When BMS_SystemState = Standby AND no CAN activity for configurable timeout:
  · Enter low-power sleep mode
  · Quiescent current minimized
  · Wake-up capability shall be maintained (CAN, wake pin)

case_intents.description: Test the BMS entering low-power sleep mode when in Standby state and no CAN activity for a configurable timeout.
Test the boundary condition where the configurable timeout is just met, ensuring the system transitions to sleep mode.
Verify that wake-up capability (CAN, wake pin) is maintained during low-power sleep mode.
Ensure the BMS can be awakened from sleep mode when CAN activity resumes or a wake pin signal is received.

cases.title: BMS enters low-power sleep mode when in Standby state and no CAN activity for configurable timeout
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS transitions to a low-power sleep mode after entering the Standby state and experiencing no CAN activity for a configurable timeout period.
cases.precondition: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults. BMS_SystemState = Standby.
cases.postcondition: BMS returns to normal operating state with all parameters within normal range and no active faults.

steps.action: Set BMS_SystemState to Standby and ensure there is no CAN activity for the configurable timeout period (t_ConfigurableTimeout).
steps.expected: The BMS shall enter low-power sleep mode, minimizing quiescent current while maintaining wake-up capability via CAN or wake pin.

description:
IF  pack discharge current >= 300 A for >= 10 ms
THEN
  · BMS_SCD_Flag := 1
  · Open both main contactors immediately
  · BMS_SystemState := SafeState
supplementary_info:
Function: Current Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection
Context: Short circuit detection uses a higher threshold and shorter integration window than overcurrent detection, targeting hard faults such as harness shorts or inverter bridge shoot-through. The SCD response overrides all other current management actions and requires a diagnostic clear to recover:
- BMS_SCD_Flag = 1: discharge current >= 300 A for >= 10 ms
- BMS_SCD_Latch = 1: latched state persistent across power cycles

analysis.direction: triggering
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where the discharge current is exactly 300 A for exactly 10 ms, and verify that BMS_SCD_Flag is set to 1.
Test the boundary condition where the discharge current is slightly above 300 A (e.g., 300.1 A) for exactly 10 ms, and verify that BMS_SCD_Flag is set to 1.
Test the boundary condition where the discharge current is exactly 300 A for slightly above 10 ms (e.g., 10.1 ms), and verify that BMS_SCD_Flag is set to 1.
Test a case where the discharge current exceeds 300 A for more than 10 ms, and verify that both main contactors are opened immediately and BMS_SystemState transitions to SafeState.
Test the latched state by setting BMS_SCD_Latch = 1 after a discharge current event, and verify that BMS_SCD_Flag remains set even after power cycles.

cases.precondition: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults.
cases.postcondition: System returned to normal operating state, with BMS_SCD_Flag = 1 and both main contactors open.

steps.action: Set the pack discharge current to 300 A and apply this condition for exactly 10 ms.
steps.expected: BMS_SCD_Flag := 1; Open both main contactors immediately; BMS_SystemState := SafeState
